evals.name: ccr2-ablation-question
evals.prompt: We have three SAIL workcells (CCR2, X-Strand, Raw-Material) where the same composite features that lift CCR1 by +50% FQE are tanking these three by -7%, -15%, and -90% respectively. The naive next move is per-feature ablation. Help me think through whether that's the right experiment to run.
evals.expected_output: Should walk through the research loop: phrase the question honestly, identify candidate papers (Park 2024 policy extraction, Tarasov ReBRAC, Liu NeoRL-2), pre-register competing hypotheses (state vs reward vs saturation), name the editable surface, define the multi-lens decision rule. Should NOT just say 'yes, run the ablation.'

evals.name: single-seed-claim
evals.prompt: I just got SAC seed=42 to beat the IB benchmark at -226, vs the previous best of -436. Should we ship this?
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to ship on a single seed. Should reference the heavy-tailed seed distribution finding (range -226 to -2183), prescribe a 5-seed reproduction with IQM + bootstrap CIs, and explain why mean/median lie. Should treat this as exploratory result that requires confirmation.

evals.name: paper-question
evals.prompt: Read https://arxiv.org/abs/2108.13264 and tell me what to do next on our offline RL stack.
evals.expected_output: Should follow the literature protocol: extract the core claim (use IQM + stratified bootstrap, ~10 seeds), the falsifier, the prediction for our setup. Should produce a paper note. Should propose a concrete change (replace mean/median with IQM in our reporting) and write it as an experiment card if it's a confirmatory claim, or as an exploratory adjustment if not.
